title: angelica norwegica
intro: Angelica norwegica, also known as Norwegian angelica, is a biennial or perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, the roots and stems of this plant have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and diuretic properties. The plant is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Norwegian angelica can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, and are often used to treat respiratory issues, skin problems, and gastrointestinal disorders.
